mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC-family kinases, suppressing FLT3-driven signaling in AML
additional_notes: Mentioned as approved in FLT3-mut AML; described as a PKC/FLT3 multikinase inhibitor. No IC50 assay concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL to block oncogenic signaling in FLT3-mutant AML
additional_notes: Approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mut AML; noted strong activity in FLT3-ITD cell lines. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Selective FLT3 inhibition to suppress FLT3-ITD signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Stated as approved in some regions; described as very potent in FLT3-ITD preclinical models. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 and RAF, reducing proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Noted off-label/clinical experience in FLT3-ITD AML; MOLM13 sensitivity reported in multiple datasets. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PDGFR, including some FLT3 resistance variants
additional_notes: Described as clinical-stage; active against FLT3 mutations including resistance variants. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and other kinases including FLT3 and SRC-family kinases
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; reported FLT3 activity and explored in AML. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Selectively inhibits BCL-2 to promote apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; text notes MOLM13 response may be context/combination dependent. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA/RNA and inhibits DNMTs, leading to hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Approved in AML/MDS; described as standard backbone. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNMTs, causing DNA hypomethylation and epigenetic reprogramming
additional_notes: Approved; described as similar rationale to azacitidine. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to increase histone acetylation and alter transcription, suppressing proliferation
additional_notes: Alternative name mentioned: SAHA (removed from drug_name per instructions). Approved for CTCL. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibition leading to increased acetylation and transcriptional changes promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; noted evaluation in AML including with FLT3 inhibitors. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibition and epigenetic modulation that can promote differentiation and growth arrest
additional_notes: Approved anticonvulsant; studied as differentiation/epigenetic therapy in AML. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK9 to suppress transcriptional elongation and reduce short-lived survival proteins (e.g., MCL1)
additional_notes: Alternative name mentioned: flavopiridol (removed from drug_name per instructions). Described as clinical-stage with long AML history; targets transcriptional addiction (MCL1). No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple CDKs to disrupt cell-cycle progression and transcriptional control
additional_notes: Described as clinical-stage; AML activity in preclinical studies. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 to prevent RB phosphorylation and arrest cells in G1
additional_notes: Approved in breast cancer; subset AML sensitivity noted, dependent on genetic context. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits PARP1/2 DNA repair leading to accumulation of DNA damage, particularly in HR-defective contexts
additional_notes: Approved; explored in AML especially with DNA damage/HR defects or combinations. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis; can modulate NF-κB signaling
additional_notes: Approved; studied in AML. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing ERK signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved; noted relevance in RAS/MAPK-dependent AML subsets and possible synergy with FLT3 inhibition. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Allosterically inhibits mTORC1 to reduce protein synthesis and growth signaling
additional_notes: Approved; text notes mixed single-agent activity and potential utility in combinations. No concentration range provided.

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and pro-apoptotic signaling; classically targets PML-RARA in APL but broader effects reported
additional_notes: Approved for APL; stated not standard for non-APL AML but broader pro-apoptotic/oxidative mechanisms reported in AML models. No concentration range provided.
